mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations, compressing profit margins and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack reduces consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is between 5.0 and 6.0.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales of existing homes and housing starts signals a broad contraction in residential demand, which reduces economic activity and corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative 60-day returns, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and supply-side momentum, which supports higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes is below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with industrial production growth.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and sell stocks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS, or the interaction effect fails to predict direction in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 over three consecutive months indicates a deteriorating labor market that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following a 3-month payroll decline exceeding 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, a quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth signals robust demand that boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during expansion phases, or predictive accuracy drops below 55% in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy where supply constraints outpace labor absorption, triggering inflation fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern does not precede negative 20-day returns, or the joint signal shows no statistical edge over individual series in walk-forward tests.
